title: A device for facilitating the removal of a safety pin of a fire extinguisher
abstract: A relieving support (17) for a safety pin (4), which prevents accidental triggering of the extinction agent in a fire extinguisher (1). The relieving support (17) prevent the operating handle (3) of the fire extinguisher (1) to be depressed towards the transport handle (2) and jam the safety pin (4) when it is to be pulled out. The relieving support (17) is arranged on the inside of the transport handle (2) of the fire extinguisher (1) and connected to the holes for the safety pin (4). The relieving support (17) is comprised of a bearing seat and a blocking device (14), which is pivotal therein, and which with a fulcrum (13) in its lower part is arranged in the bearing seat (6). The abutment of the blocking device (14) against the inside of the operating handle (3) of the fire extinguisher (1) is angled so that the blocking device (14) can be turned away when the operating handle (3) is depressed, after the safety pin (5) has been pulled out. When the safety pin (5) is removed,the operating handle (3) can affect the triggering valve of the fire extinguisher (1), sothat the extinction can be commenced.
claims: Claims laims 1 . A device for facilitating removal of a safety pin (3) of a fire extinguisher (1 ), which is mainly comprised of a container for fire extinction agent, a transport handle (2) fixedly attached to the container, an operating handle (3) pivotally attached in relation to the transport handle, arranged to be operated by a pivotal movement and thereby activating a discharge device (23) having a discharge valve (25) for the discharge of the fire extinction agent, wherein the safety pin (4) is arranged to block the operating handle against accidental activation in a locked position, characterized in that a relieving support (17/51 ) is arranged between the transport handle (2) and the operating handle (3) in the form of a bearing seat which is fixedly arranged in relation to one of the handles and a blocking device (14/45), which is pivotally arranged in relation to the bearing seat around a pivot shaft (12/52), and which in a blocking position is arranged to lie with a contact surface (21 ) against a guide surface (30) on the other handle and to be blocked by the safety pin, wherein the guide surface and the contact surface have a chosen mutual geometry, which creates only a small torque on the blocking device (14/45) around its pivot shaft (12/52), when a force is applied between the operating handle and the transport handle, so that such a small force is created against the safety pin that it can be pulled out easily, but large enough for the blocking device to barely being turned away. 2. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the transport handle (3) with is guide surface (30) is angled somewhat outwards from the transport handle (2) with a small positive angle (V2) to an imaginary touching line (34), which extends through a touching point (32) of the contact surface (21 ) of the blocking device (14) against the guide surface and perpendicularly to a radius (33) between the touching point and the pivot shaft (12/52) of the blocking device, and thereby creates the small force, which is chosen with a magnitude which overcomes counteracting frictional forces and does not prevent the removal of the safety pin (4). 3. The device according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the relieving support (17) is firmly fixed to the transport handle (2) and in that the relieving support (17) is comprised of two mirror-inverted rims (6) and between these a pivotal blocking device (14), which in a fulcrum (13) in its lower part is applied in the rims (6), and the two mirror-inverted rims (6) are designed with holes (7) for a safety pin (4) and the blocking device (14) is on each side in its lower part integrated with semicircular pins (16), and the pins (16) of the blocking device (14) are arranged to be inserted down into a slot (9) on each inside (19) between the two rims (6), and the pins (16) of the blocking device (14) form a pivot shaft (12) around which the blocking device (14) is arranged to be turned, and the holes (7) of the rims (6) matches the semicircular slot (15) in the blocking device (14), so that the safety pin (4) can be passed through when the blocking device (14) is turned up, and the safety pin (4) is arranged to be applied through the holes in both rims (6) and the semicircular slot (15) in the blocking device (14) and thereby locks the blocking device (14) and prevents thereby the operating handle (3) to be depressed and accidentally trigger the discharge of the extinction agent in the fire extinguisher (1 ). 4. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the mirror-inverted rims (6) of the relieving support (17) have an outer side (18) which is set against the inner side of the transport handle, and the rim (6) is provided with a hole (7) for a safety pin (4) and a bushing (8) is formed around the hole (7) on the outside (18) of the rim (6), and the safety pin has a diameter which is smaller than the holes in the transport handle, through which the pin extends in the blocking position and the bushing (8) fits on the inside into the hole in the transport handle (2) of the fire extinguisher (1 ), which is intended for the safety pin (4) and becomes thereby firmly fixed in position against the transport handle (2), and a resilient flap (10) is formed at the forward edge of the rim (6) designed to clamp the rim (6) against the inside of the transport handle through the valve housing (25) of the discharge device (23), when the bushing (8) of the rim (6) is fitted into the hole of the transport handle for the safety pin. 5. The device according to claim 1 characterized in that the insides (19) of the mirror-inverted rims (6) of the relieving support are provided with a slot (9), down into which the pins (16) of the blocking device (14) are intended to be inserted, and the slot (9) is, at its lower part, designed with a round recess (13) , which forms a fulcrum for the pivot shaft (12) of the blocking device (14), and in the lower edge of the rim (6) a fulcrum (1 1 ) is formed, which rests against the inside of the transport handle, directly underneath the pivot shaft (12). 6. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the blocking device (14) of the relieving support (17) in the middle has a semicircular slot (15) intended for a safety pin (4), and the relieving support (17) is fixed in the transport handle (2) of the fire extinguisher (1 ) and the blocking device (14) is locked by the safety pin (4) so that the operating handle (3) is prevented from being depressed and jam the safety pin (4), and the small force acting on the safety pin (4) is a fraction of the force (20) with which the operating handle (3) is depressed, and this results in that the safety pin (4) without difficulties can be pulled out, whereby the blocking device (14) is turned away by the operating handle (3) and the extinction can be commenced. 7. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the bearing seat is fixed to the transport handle (2) and in that the guide surface (30) is arranged at the operating handle (3). 8. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the bearing seat is fixed to the operating handle and in that the guide surface is arranged at the transport handle. 9. The device according to claim 1 , characterized in that the hole (7) in the rim (6) and the semicircular slot (15) in the blocking device (14) are formed in such a way that they do not jam the safety pin (4) because of tolerances and resiliency of the materials 10. The device according to claim 1 or 2, characterized in that the bearing seat is comprised of a basic part (40), which is fixedly attached on the discharge device (23) of the fire extinguisher. 1 1 The device according to claim 10, characterized in that the basic part (40) has a through opening (41 ) for the fastening of the basic part adapted to the valve housing (25) of the discharge device (23).
